mnemonics: R-GPD: Reputation Guards Precious Data. Think of a famous, trusted security guard (Reputation) protecting a valuable data center vault.
keyPoints: Reputation is a security metric based on the historical behavior and trustworthiness of a user, service, or data center component.
It is used to dynamically guide security policies, allocating more resources to monitor or restrict entities with poor reputations.
This approach enables proactive and adaptive protection, moving beyond traditional static security measures.
commonMistakes: Equating reputation solely with popularity or traffic volume, rather than a calculated measure of trust and past behavior.
Assuming a good reputation means absolute safety, leading to complacency and a failure to maintain continuous monitoring.
